title: Object Relationships
learning_outcomes: Describe structural characteristics of Object Relationships.

Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for Association and simple dependencies definitions, Aggregation (weak ownership, shared lifecycles), Composition (strong ownership, parent-child bound lifecycles), Dependency injection configurations.

content_sections.title: Object Relationships
content_sections.content:
### Object-Oriented Programming Paradigms
OOP structures software around classes and objects, prioritizing loose coupling and high cohesion.

#### Dynamic Method Dispatch
When invoking overridden methods, the compiler cannot determine the implementation at build time. The JVM resolves this at runtime via **vTables (Virtual Method Tables)**. Every class holds a vTable mapping method signatures to physical memory addresses. Method invocations fetch the address from the vTable of the active runtime object instance.

#### Composition vs Inheritance
*   **Inheritance (Is-A):** Couples subclasses to base classes, violating encapsulation (Fragile Base Class problem).
*   **Composition (Has-A):** Delegates behavior to helper classes. Allows swapping behaviors dynamically at runtime and maintains class independence.

#### SOLID Design Principles
1.  **Single Responsibility:** A class should have only one reason to change.
2.  **Open/Closed:** Open for extension, closed for modification.
3.  **Liskov Substitution:** Subtypes must be substitutable for their base types.
4.  **Interface Segregation:** Clients should not depend on interfaces they do not use.
5.  **Dependency Inversion:** Depend on abstractions, not concretions.

### Depth Analysis: Object Relationships
In production configurations, we analyze how Association and simple dependencies definitions, Aggregation (weak ownership, shared lifecycles), Composition (strong ownership, parent-child bound lifecycles), Dependency injection configurations affects performance parameters. We evaluate memory layouts and consensus thresholds.

callouts.title: Common Trap in Object Relationships
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Object Relationships is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.

callouts.title: Production Scaling for Object Relationships
callouts.content: Production servers implement strict resource isolation and cache alignments to prevent performance regressions when running Object Relationships loops.
